title: delphinium emarginatum subsp. nevadense
intro: Delphinium emarginatum subsp. nevadense, also known as the Nevada delphinium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its tall spikes of blue or white flowers. The Nevada delphinium has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the Nevada delphinium may include infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
